mnemonics: SUSPENDERS hold up THREADS: Use suspend() and resume() to manage thread execution, but they are deprecated because they are unsafe like old, broken suspenders.
keyPoints: The suspend() method pauses a thread's execution.
The resume() method restarts a thread that was suspended.
Both methods are deprecated because they are inherently unsafe and can lead to deadlocks.
commonMistakes: Using the deprecated suspend() and resume() methods in new code instead of modern synchronization techniques.
Not realizing that suspend() holds the thread's lock, which can easily cause a deadlock if another thread tries to access that same locked resource.
Assuming a suspended thread will be safe or predictable, when its state is actually left inconsistent.
